title: Persist associated dataset stats for score-page UI consumption
description:
## Summary
Persist associated-dataset stats at build time for UI consumption.

## Purpose
Allow score-page dataset visibility without expensive per-view parquet scans.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Newly curated associated datasets persist row count and label distribution metadata.
- Persist class list and curation policy metadata used for build.
- Score-page UI path can read these stats directly.
- No on-demand parquet parsing is required for this feature path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting persistence work for dataset stats metadata. Plan: store row_count/label_distribution/class_list/policy in associated-dataset datasource version configuration at build time, with tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented build-time dataset stats persistence for associated dataset curation. Changes: datasource-version YAML configuration now includes dataset_stats payload (row_count, label_distribution, class_list_used, curation_policy, balance_applied, and before/after distribution for balanced window builds). Wired both curation paths: score dataset-curate window flow and explicit feedback-id flow populate dataset_stats at creation time. Added/updated tests to assert stats are emitted and threaded through: curation_test and datasets_test. Verification: pytest -q plexus/cli/dataset/curation_test.py plexus/cli/score/scores_dataset_curate_test.py tests/scores/test_score_validation.py -q => pass; py_compile on changed modules => pass.
